name: RMS Rhone
validation_source: Blue Water Divers, DiveBVI.com, PADI dive site guides

tags.notes: Maximum depth 26m (85ft). Bow at 24m, stern at 9m. Very open wreck with few entrapment hazards. World-class Caribbean wreck dive.

name: The Indians
validation_source: ScubaBoard BVI diving discussions, dive operator sites

tags.notes: Four rocky pinnacles. Shallow side 3m coral garden, deeper side steep wall to 11m. Excellent marine life including snapper, dolphins, turtles. Can have strong currents.

name: The Caves at Norman Island
validation_source: BVI-Dive-Map.com, epic yacht charters, local dive guides

tags.notes: Three water-level caves at Norman Island. First cave extends ~27m. Popular for snorkeling and shallow diving. Great for beginners and snorkelers.
Deepwater pinnacle 1.5km SE of Norman Island. Depths 6-31m. Craggy top with fire coral. Distinctive 'sink hole' feature. Pelagic fish and sharks. Watch for heavy fire coral.

name: Blonde Rock
validation_source: BVIDiving.com, Sail Caribbean Divers, dive operator sites

tags.notes: Shallow pinnacle between Salt Island and Dead Chest Island. Rises from 18m to 5m. Complex system of tunnels, overhangs, undercuts. Abundant lobsters. Weather-dependent.
Off Ginger Island south shore. Spur and groove reef 11-21m. Outstanding visibility. Mushroom-shaped coral formations. Eagle rays, stingrays, turtles common. Easy navigation.

name: Wreck Alley
validation_source: PADI Dive Site guides, ZuBlu, BVIDiving.com, Reef Smart Guides

tags.notes: Four scuttled wrecks (Pat, Beata, Mary L, Island Seal) off Cooper Island. Depths ~27m. Garden eels, rays, snappers, groupers, moray eels. Thriving artificial reef.

name: Anegada Reef
validation_source: PADI dive guides, Dive and Cruise, DiveScore

tags.notes: Part of 29km Horseshoe Reef - largest barrier reef in Caribbean. Pristine reefs, nurse sharks, rays, turtles. Access limited to those with local guide due to shallow and coral-dense conditions.

name: The Chimney at Great Dog Island

tags.notes: Two rocky formations create a chimney gap. Protected northwest of Great Dog Island. Depths 9-12m. Shrimps, damselfish, parrotfish, scorpionfish. Famous Jacques Cousteau site.
East of Cooper Island. Ridge rises from 24m. Open water site with pillar corals, black corals, sea fans. Pelagic fish (cobia, African pompano). Can have strong currents - heed dive briefing.

Three dramatic step walls on Ginger Island southwest: 12m, 20m, 29m. Tube sponges, sea fans, corals. Protected from currents. White sand reflects sunlight creating magical scenery. Suitable for all levels.

name: Playgrounds Anegada
validation_source: PADI dive guides, Anegada diving information

tags.notes: Shallow reef site around Anegada. Part of pristine Horseshoe Reef system. Excellent for beginner divers. Requires local guide access due to shallow waters and coral maze characteristics.
